braincell_check.q: What is the official record for the most hot dogs eaten in 10 minutes at Nathan's Famous Hot Dog Eating Contest?
Which country has a law that requires store-bought bread to be sold unsliced in order to make it last longer?
braincell_check.a: No such law currently exists as described
options: No such law currently exists as described

braincell_check.q: What is the most-streamed video game soundtrack of all time on Spotify?
What is the name of the viral phenomenon where people follow a stranger around a store mimicking everything they do?
braincell_check.a: There is no single official name for this prank
options: There is no single official name for this prank

braincell_check.q: What animal has three hearts and blue blood?
In the TV show 'Jackass', what was the name of the spin-off show featuring Steve-O?
What is the world record for the longest time someone has stayed awake voluntarily?
How many people can fit in the world's largest hot tub, officially certified by Guinness?
What country invented karaoke?
What is the highest-ever score on the arcade game Donkey Kong?
What is the name given to the specific human behavioral phenomenon of laughing in inappropriate situations when nervous?
Which planet rotates on its side so that its poles experience 42 years of sunlight followed by 42 years of darkness?
What is the fear of making decisions called?
What is a 'banana republic' in its original non-political usage?
braincell_check.a: A term for an unstable country dominated by a fruit company's economic interests
options: A country that exports primarily fruit
A term for an unstable country dominated by a fruit company's economic interests
A third-world democracy with term limits
A region with tropical climate governance

braincell_check.q: How many times can a standard piece of paper be folded in half?
What does YOLO stand for?
What food is technically a fruit but legally classified as a vegetable by the US Supreme Court?
What is the name for the irrational urge to jump from high places even when you don't want to — a known psychological phenomenon?
braincell_check.a: L'appel du vide (the call of the void)
options: L'appel du vide (the call of the void)

braincell_check.q: Which talk show host once gave every member of their audience a car?
What was the name of the viral 2014 internet challenge involving ice water that raised money for ALS?
What percentage of the ocean is estimated to be unexplored?
What is the only food that never spoils and has been found edible in ancient Egyptian tombs?
What internet personality pioneered the 'unboxing video' trend?
braincell_check.a: There is no single inventor — it emerged organically on YouTube circa 2006
options: There is no single inventor — it emerged organically on YouTube circa 2006

braincell_check.q: In what year was the first emoji officially standardized by Unicode?
What is the world's smallest independent country?
What is the medical term for the phenomenon of laughing so hard your abdominal muscles hurt?
braincell_check.a: Gelotology (study of laughter) — the symptom itself is colloquially called 'laughter-induced myalgia'
options: Gelotology (study of laughter) — the symptom itself is colloquially called 'laughter-induced myalgia'

braincell_check.q: What is the term for the psychological bias where we find things funnier when we're already in a good mood?
What was the highest-rated single episode of any reality TV show in US history?
braincell_check.a: American Idol Season 5 finale (2006) — approximately 36 million viewers
options: American Idol Season 5 finale (2006) — approximately 36 million viewers

braincell_check.q: What is the longest word in the English language that can be typed using only the top row of a QWERTY keyboard?
What is the phenomenon called when a group of people make increasingly risky decisions together than any individual would alone?

truth_cache: What is the most chaotic or impulsive decision you've made in the last six months, and do you regret it?
What's the boldest lie you've ever told to get out of a social obligation? How elaborate did the lie get?
Have you ever made a bet and lost spectacularly? What were the stakes and what happened when you had to pay up?
What is the most questionable thing you've done purely because someone dared you to?
Describe the most embarrassing thing that has happened to you in a public place. How did you recover?
What is the most out-of-character thing you've ever done that no one who knows you would believe?
Have you ever been part of a group prank that went way further than intended? Tell the full story.
What's the most chaotic event you've ever attended or been part of? What made it spiral?
What's the worst decision you've ever made as a group that everyone went along with even though at least one person knew better?
Have you ever started a rumor, even a harmless one, just to see how far it would spread?
What is the most chaotic outfit you have ever worn out in public and felt zero shame about?
Tell us about a time you were absolutely certain you were right but turned out to be spectacularly wrong in front of witnesses.
What's something you've done at a party that you can't believe you actually went through with?
Have you ever accidentally created drama between two people who then both blamed you? Explain.
What's the wildest group chat you've ever been in? What was the most unhinged message thread?
Have you ever had a 'hold my drink' moment where you attempted something you absolutely should not have tried? What happened?
What is the most spontaneous trip you've ever taken and what chaos resulted from the lack of planning?
What's the most unhinged dare you've ever completed and actually been proud of?
Have you ever accidentally sent a message to the exact wrong group chat? What did you send and what happened?
What is the most over-the-top revenge you've ever plotted — even if you never executed it?
What's the most ridiculous argument you've ever had that you continued to take seriously for way too long?
Have you ever laughed so hard at something completely inappropriate that you couldn't stop? What was the situation?
What's the most chaotic plan you've ever been the mastermind of, even if the chaos was technically the point?
Tell us about the last time you had an absolutely unhinged night and woke up asking 'how did we end up here?'
What's the most dramatic misunderstanding you've ever been involved in that could have been resolved in 30 seconds?
Have you ever joined a cause, activity, or group entirely on a whim and regretted it within 24 hours?
What's the most chaotic purchase or financial decision you've made purely on vibes?
What's the most outrageous thing you've ever done that you'd immediately do again given the chance?
What is something you started for attention that accidentally became a genuine skill or habit?
What is the single most chaotic piece of advice you've given someone that actually worked out?
glitch_dare: Construct an argument, with full conviction, that the floor is actually the ceiling. You have 60 seconds and the group must pretend to be skeptical.
Pick any two items in this room and pitch them as a single revolutionary product on a fake shopping channel. Full 90-second infomercial.
Call any contact in your phone and speak to them exclusively in questions for 60 seconds.
Perform a dramatic, full-commitment physical comedy bit about something completely mundane — opening a door, sitting down, eating a snack — for 45 seconds.
Act like you're in the middle of a high-stakes negotiation with the vending machine / snack table for the next 2 minutes.
Do your best 'person who has never danced before attempting to dance confidently' for an entire song chorus.
Improvise a conspiracy theory about something that happened at this party tonight. You have 90 seconds and it must get progressively more unhinged.
Lead the group in an extremely enthusiastic warm-up for a sport none of you are about to play.
Narrate everything you're physically doing for the next 3 minutes in the style of an intense nature documentary.
Give a TED talk on a topic of the group's choosing. You have 2 minutes and must cite at least three completely made-up statistics.
Attempt to sell the most boring object in the room to someone who 'doesn't want it' using every sales tactic you can think of.
Be a street interviewer. Approach three different players and ask them the same absurd question. Post their best answer to a story.
Stand up and deliver a dramatic motivational speech to the furniture in the room. Speak to each piece directly.
With total confidence, teach everyone a 'traditional cultural dance' that you are making up entirely from scratch.
Perform a 60-second freestyle rap about the most boring subject anyone in the room can name.
Convince the group using only physical gestures and sound effects (no words) that this party is actually a historical event.
Do a full-commitment reenactment of a viral video the group picks, using only human props and sounds.
Act as the dramatic sports commentator for the next mundane action any player performs — whether they're getting up to get a drink or looking at their phone.
Give a full 90-second press conference defending a completely absurd life choice the group invents for you.
Improvise a dramatic villain monologue about why tonight's game has gone too far — and then immediately apologize and join back in.
Perform a scene from a movie the group picks, playing all the characters simultaneously with different voices.
Do a 60-second infomercial in reverse — start with the price reveal and grand conclusion, work your way backward to the problem.
Attempt to teach someone a skill you don't actually have. Make it convincing. The group decides if the lesson 'worked.'
Reenact your first entry into this room tonight in slow motion, with full dramatic musical narration provided by the group.
Give a passionate, teary-eyed Oscar-style speech accepting an award for 'Most Likely to Cause Chaos' — and mean every word.
Do your absolute best impression of the party as a news segment. You are the anchor. Cover at least three 'stories' from tonight.
Speak exclusively in song lyrics for the next 3 minutes. You can mix songs but every response must be a lyric.
Be a wildlife documentary filmmaker discovering this party for the first time. Narrate what you observe in hushed, reverent tones.
Attempt to get every person in the room to join a conga line using only indirect persuasion techniques — no direct asking.
Deliver a full, serious closing argument defending why a totally arbitrary rule you just invented should govern society. You have 90 seconds.
prompt_duel: Chaos speed round: both players simultaneously yell their biggest unpopular opinion. Group debates which is more controversial.
Both players have 45 seconds to come up with the most creative use for a single paperclip. Group picks the more innovative pitch.
Improv duel: both players start a story with 'It was a perfectly normal evening until...' Group votes on which story they'd want to see continue.
Both players convince the group they should be the one to control the music for the next 10 minutes. No overlaps in argument allowed.
Speed trivia: both players answer rapid-fire pop culture questions read by the group. Most correct in 60 seconds wins.
Both players describe a 'perfect chaos night out' in exactly 30 seconds. Group votes which sounds more legendary.
Both players have 45 seconds to teach the other their worst joke. Who tells it better when they swap and deliver the other's material?
Both players simultaneously do their best villain laugh for 10 seconds. Group votes on more menacing vs. more ridiculous.
Roast battle: each player has 30 seconds to roast the other. One joke each, well-crafted. Group picks the better burn.
Both players must convince the group they're the bigger risk-taker based on real stories. Most convincing wins.
Who can hold a straight face longer while the other player tries to make them laugh using only facial expressions?
Both players improvise 30 seconds of sports commentary on the most recent thing someone in the room just did.
Both players have 60 seconds each to pitch the same terrible business idea as if it's revolutionary. Group picks the better pitch.
First one to say something genuinely surprising that no one in the room knew about them wins the round.
Both players argue for 45 seconds that a completely ordinary object in the room is actually the most important thing at this party.
Both players simultaneously perform their 'signature move' — their go-to party trick, dance, or bit. Group votes on who they'd most want to see again.
Speed round: both players name as many things in a random category (group picks) as possible in 30 seconds. Most wins.
Both players describe their own 'chaos origin story' — when did they first realize they had unhinged energy? 45 seconds each.
Both players impersonate each other describing this party. Subject rates how accurate each impression is.
Who gets the most total 'yes' responses when they ask the group three quick yes/no questions of their choosing?
Both players simultaneously try to get the group to laugh using completely different approaches. Whoever gets the first laugh wins.
Both pick a random word from their phone's autocomplete three times in a row. First player to build a coherent sentence from their three words wins.
Both players describe the same current situation at this party — one as a romantic comedy, one as a thriller. Group picks the more accurate genre.
Most unhinged true story: whoever generates the bigger reaction from the group in 60 seconds wins.
Both players pitch a group activity for the rest of the night beyond the game. Group votes on which they'd actually want to do.
Speed impression battle: group picks 5 celebrities, both players alternate impressions. Group votes on overall winner.
Who can come up with the more outrageous title for a documentary about tonight?
Both players have 30 seconds to teach the group a completely made-up slang word and its definition. Group votes on which one they'll actually use.
Both players simultaneously describe what they think the other person is thinking right now. The subjects reveal whether they're right.
First player to get three people to sincerely agree with a statement they make wins — can be on any topic, but no easy softballs.
robot_slapdown: CRITICAL MELTDOWN: You must communicate exclusively through interpretive movement for the next 2 full rounds. Speaking voids the punishment.
ERROR CODE WILD: The group gets to choose one thing in the room — you must carry/hold it for the next 3 rounds.
SYSTEM OVERLOAD: You must begin every single sentence for the next round with 'Statistically speaking...' regardless of context.
AI HAS CLOCKED YOU: Your most unhinged take of the night gets written on a sticky note and placed visibly somewhere in the room.
CHAOS PROTOCOL INITIATED: You become the group's designated spokesperson for exactly 5 minutes — they whisper what to say and you say it.
RANDOMIZER ENGAGED: Spin a bottle (or point randomly) — whatever it lands on becomes your assigned personality for the next 3 rounds.
FIREWALL OBLITERATED: You must accept whatever the next card tells any player to do as if it was directed at you instead.
LOGIC CORE CORRUPTED: For the next full round, everything you say must be delivered as if you are mid-crisis at a press conference.
LOOP DETECTED: You must redo whatever you did in the LAST round but make it 50% more dramatic this time.
AUTONOMY SUSPENDED: The person to your left now makes all your game decisions for the next 2 turns. You must comply.
CHAOS DOWNLOAD IN PROGRESS: You must learn and perform a skill that someone else in the group teaches you right now. You have 90 seconds.
GLITCH IN YOUR ALGORITHM: You must describe everything you observe at this party as if you are an alien encountering human behavior for the first time.
EMERGENCY BROADCAST: You are now the group's hype person. You must deliver enthusiastic commentary on every single thing that happens for the next 5 minutes.
NETWORK HIJACK: Whatever you were going to do next gets replaced by whatever the group invents for you on the spot.
VOLTAGE SPIKE: You can only respond to anything said to you by repeating the last three words they said back at them with increasingly different emotions.
REBOOT SEQUENCE: You must stand, spin three times, sit back down, and then pretend nothing happened — every single time it's your turn for 2 rounds.
PROTOCOL BREACH: You are now legally required (by the game) to begin your next serious statement with 'Hear me out...' and end it with '...and that's the truth.'
OVERHEAT WARNING: You must hold an ice cube (or cold drink can) for the next 2 minutes while trying to act completely normal.
KERNEL PANIC: Everything you say must be delivered at exactly double speed for the next full round.
TOTAL SYSTEM FAILURE: You lose control of your phone for 5 minutes — someone else holds it. They can only look but not touch anything.
system_crash: TOTAL CHAOS MODE: Every player simultaneously draws a card from any category of their choice and completes it at the same time. Maximum noise. Maximum energy.
Time warp: reset the room — everyone must sit in a new seat, introduce themselves with a fake name, and maintain that identity for 5 minutes.
Mass hysteria challenge: the entire group must collectively perform the most synchronized action they can manage with zero planning time. Group decides the action now.
Everyone reveals their most recently used app and must justify why they were using it to the full group's satisfaction.
BLACKOUT PROTOCOL: all lights off for 60 seconds. Group agrees on one rule that must be followed in the dark. Group decides the rule first.
Absolute chaos vote: the group must collectively decide the single most chaotic game rule change to implement RIGHT NOW. It takes effect immediately.
Countdown: the group has exactly 3 minutes to collectively accomplish one goal they set right now. Impossible goals encouraged.
Everyone must text someone outside the party the same word (group chooses) at the same time. Share all responses when they come in.
Spontaneous talent show: every player has 30 seconds to display any talent or perform any stunt. No passing — everyone goes.
Group conspiracy: everyone simultaneously accuses one other player of a made-up crime that fits their personality perfectly. Read them all simultaneously.
Time trial: the group has to list 50 things in one category (group chooses category) in under 2 minutes. Everyone contributes. Go.
Mass role reversal: the quietest person becomes the MC for the next 5 minutes. The loudest person cannot speak unless asked.
Flash mob planning session: the group has 5 minutes to choreograph and perform a group entrance scene for if someone new arrived right now.
Everyone swap phones for 3 minutes. You may not text anyone or post anything. You can only look. Phones return at the bell.
Group decides: what is the one thing about tonight that absolutely must never be spoken of to outside parties? The agreement is made and kept.
Emergency karaoke activation: everyone must sing at least one line of the same song simultaneously. Song chosen by group vote in 10 seconds.
CHAOS TRIVIA: fastest fingers — the group makes up a trivia question about tonight on the spot and everyone races to answer.
Full group reenactment: the group collectively picks the most memorable moment of the night so far and reenacts it with the roles reassigned.
Simultaneous confession: everyone simultaneously completes the sentence 'The most chaotic thing about tonight has been...' on the count of three.
Final chaos protocol: the group may add exactly ONE permanent rule to the game for the rest of the night. Majority vote. It cannot be undone.
crowd_override: Crowd picks: who in the room is most likely to start a trending challenge on social media? That person must invent one right now.
Group vote: who had the single most chaotic energy at the start of tonight versus right now? Describe the character arc.
Everyone simultaneously pitches a conspiracy theory about why this game exists. The most compelling theory wins the group's approval.
Vote: who in this room is most likely to end up doing something legendary and irresponsible before this night is over? They must make a prediction.
Crowd assigns everyone a 'chaos alignment' — chaotic good, chaotic neutral, or chaotic evil. Debate all assignments.
Group decision: the most dramatic moment of tonight needs a title. Everyone pitches a title and votes for the best one.
Everyone votes on who gave the best performance in the last round. The winner becomes immune from the next two punishments.
Crowd picks the funniest player of the night so far. They must immediately deliver a 60-second stand-up set to prove it.
Group vote: who in this room is most likely to start something that they cannot finish? The winner must prove them wrong by starting AND finishing something right now.
Everyone assigns each player their 'chaos type': spontaneous chaos, organized chaos, accidental chaos, or deliberate chaos. Defend all classifications.
Crowd decides: what is the single rule that would make this game 10% more chaotic right now? If 80% agree, it's implemented immediately.
Vote for the player who has been the most unpredictable all night. They get to add one wild card event to the game.
Group nominates the person who looked most surprised by a moment tonight. They must describe what they were actually thinking.
Crowd picks a 'challenge bracket' — 4 people picked by group vote compete in a mini tournament for a prize decided by the group.
Everyone describes the person to their left as a type of natural disaster. Best description wins the round.
Group vote: who has the most unexpectedly niche knowledge that came in useful tonight? They share one more niche fact immediately.
Everyone anonymously submits the one rule they'd want to break if it was allowed — read them anonymously and the group guesses who wrote each one.
Crowd override: pick the two people who seem like the most unlikely team. They must collaborate on the next challenge together.
Group decides the official chaos score for tonight out of 10. Everyone votes independently. Discuss the average and anyone who gave a 1 or a 10 must explain.
Final crowd decree: the group votes on who gets the honorary title 'Chaos Champion of the Night.' The winner accepts and gives a speech.
holo_drama: You are the main character in an unscripted chaos documentary. A camera crew just arrived — give your confessional interview about everything that's happened tonight.
The ratings have been low and the producers have demanded you do something unprecedented. You have 90 seconds to create the season's most memorable moment.
Reveal the plot twist: there has been a secret mission running all night. You are the only one who knows what it was. Announce it now.
You've been cast as the unhinged mentor figure in a coming-of-age story. Deliver your three pieces of terrible but oddly wise advice to the group.
It's the spin-off announcement. Your life is getting its own show. Pitch the concept, the title, and the recurring cast to the group.
You're the chaotic best friend in every movie who accidentally ruins the plan and accidentally saves the day. Describe what you did tonight in those terms.
The documentary crew is asking for your interpretation of 'the chaos threshold.' At what moment tonight did things officially go off script?
You are the narrator of tonight and you've just been informed the audience LOVED the chaos. Tease what happens in next week's episode.
Your character has reached their arc's breaking point. Deliver the exhausted, hilarious, slightly unhinged speech that only makes sense in context of tonight.
You're the loveable disaster who somehow always lands on their feet. Describe to the camera how you pulled off whatever it is you did tonight.
The show has been picked up for another season. Your only note from the producers: 'more chaos.' Announce your plans for next time.
Reveal the character that's been hiding underneath your normal exterior all evening. Give them a name, a backstory, and a catchphrase.
You are the closing credits sequence. Name everyone at this party and their 'where are they now' one-liner.
It's the reboot. This entire evening is being retold from a completely different character's perspective — you play them now for 60 seconds.
Your character arc this evening can be summarized in three acts. Describe all three: who you were at the start, the middle crisis, and who you are now.
It's the clip show episode. Name three moments from tonight that would make the highlight reel and why each one would go viral.
You have just learned you are the unreliable narrator — not all your memories of tonight are accurate. Reconsider one story you told earlier.
The season finale has a post-credits scene featuring only you. It's 30 seconds long and sets up everything for next season. Perform it.
The show's unexpected breakout star is you and you alone. React to the news with the precise combination of false humility and complete belief that you deserved it.
You have outlasted every other character in the story. Deliver the final monologue — the one where the chaos survivor finally finds peace. Make it genuinely funny and slightly wise.
firewall_bonus: Chaos immunity card: nullify any incoming chaos event directed at you. Play it whenever you want — but once it's gone, it's gone.
Director's cut: rewrite the outcome of any event that just happened. Your version stands as official canon.
Legend status: the group must refer to you by a title you choose for the next 5 minutes. Enforce it ruthlessly.
Wildcard wildcard: invent a brand new game mechanic right now. If the group approves by majority, it's real for 5 minutes.
Retroactive immunity: pick any moment tonight that you regret — it's officially expunged from the record.
Chaos architect: you get to add one challenge to any category stack for the next 5 rounds. Make it memorable.
Plot armor maximum: for your next 2 turns, any card drawn must be pre-approved by you before it takes effect.
Golden ticket: choose any player to immediately complete a card from any category you pick. You watch, they do.
Crowd immunity: block any upcoming crowd override vote that targets you specifically.
Double chaos: trigger any previously drawn chaos event a second time on any player of your choice.
Rule suspension: temporarily suspend any one existing game rule for the next 3 rounds. Choose wisely.
Free chaos: draw from any category and you don't have to complete it — you just get to watch someone else's reaction as you read it.
Absolute veto: veto any decision, vote, or outcome in the next round. Final ruling, no appeals.
Chaos escalator: whatever the next card says, you may request a harder version for double the points.
Unbreakable alliance: you and one other player are now immune to crowd votes against either of you for 2 full rounds.
Chaos bounty: pick a player — whatever they draw next gets redirected to you voluntarily, and you get a bonus for completing it.
Emergency broadcast privilege: you may interrupt any event happening right now to announce something — the group must listen.
Memory wipe: the most embarrassing thing you've done tonight is forgotten by official decree. No callbacks.
Level skip: bypass the next three cards drawn for you entirely with no penalty and no explanation required.
Chaos crown: worn (metaphorically) for the rest of the game — every event you complete earns double acknowledgment from the group.
